---
title: "Theorem 7.2 — Score CLT"
lean_name: "HansenEconometrics.scoreVector_sampleCrossMoment_tendstoInDistribution_multivariateGaussian"
chapter: 7
---

## Textbook statement

For an i.i.d. sample with score vector $e_i X_i$, assume the Chapter 7 score
CLT conditions: the score has mean zero, the relevant second moments exist, and
the score covariance matrix

$$
\Omega = \mathbb{E}[e_i^2 X_i X_i^\top]
$$

is finite. Then the scaled sample score converges in distribution:

$$
\sqrt{n}\,\hat g_n(e)
= \frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^n e_i X_i
\Rightarrow N(0,\Omega).
$$

In Lean, the assumptions are bundled as
[`ScoreCLTConditions μ X e`](../../assumptions/chapter7.qmd), with the
textbook-compatible alias
[`SampleCLTAssumption72 μ X e`](../../assumptions/chapter7.qmd). The theorem
is stated for the stacked sample cross moment
`sampleCrossMoment (stackRegressors X n ω) (stackErrors e n ω)`.

### Proof sketch

The Lean proof follows the textbook route but makes the Cramer-Wold step
explicit.

1. Fix a direction $a \in \mathbb{R}^k$.
2. Apply Mathlib's real-valued i.i.d. CLT to the scalar sequence
   $(e_i X_i)^\top a$.
3. Rewrite the scalar variance as the quadratic form $a^\top \Omega a$.
4. Use the reusable Cramer-Wold bridge to assemble the vector-valued Gaussian
   convergence.

## Lean statement

The public vector theorem lives in
`HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Normality.lean`:

| Lean endpoint | Role |
|---|---|
| [`scoreProjection_sum_tendstoInDistribution_gaussian`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Normality.lean#L61) | scalar real CLT for $(1/\sqrt n)\sum (e_iX_i)^\top a$ |
| [`scoreProjection_sampleCrossMoment_tendstoInDistribution_gaussian_covariance`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Normality.lean#L129) | same scalar CLT in sample-cross-moment notation with variance $a^\top\Omega a$ |
| [`scoreProjection_sampleCrossMoment_tendstoInDistribution_gaussian_covariance_all`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Normality.lean#L155) | all scalar projections, ready for Cramer-Wold |
| [`scoreVector_sampleCrossMoment_tendstoInDistribution_multivariateGaussian`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Normality.lean#L232) | public vector score CLT |

::: {.lean-hover-panel}
```{=html}
<pre><code><span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Public Chapter 7 vector score CLT.">theorem scoreVector_sampleCrossMoment_tendstoInDistribution_multivariateGaussian</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="The sample space is a probability space.">{μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="The regressor and error processes; observation i at sample point ω gives X i ω and e i ω.">{X : ℕ → Ω → (k → ℝ)} {e : ℕ → Ω → ℝ}</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Bundled Theorem 7.2 assumptions: i.i.d. score structure, zero mean, moment bounds, and covariance information.">(h : ScoreCLTConditions μ X e)</span> :
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Conclusion: sqrt(n) times the sample cross moment converges in distribution to a centered multivariate Gaussian with covariance Ω.">TendstoInDistribution
      (fun (n : ℕ) ω =>
        Real.sqrt (n : ℝ) •
          sampleCrossMoment (stackRegressors X n ω) (stackErrors e n ω))
      atTop
      (fun z : EuclideanSpace ℝ k => z.ofLp)
      (fun _ => μ)
      (multivariateGaussian 0 (scoreCovarianceMatrix μ X e))</span>
</code></pre>
```
:::

### Reading the statement

| Lean | Math | What it gives us |
|---|---|---|
| `{μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]` | $(\Omega,\mu)$ | **Given:** the probability space on which the sample is defined. |
| `{X : ℕ → Ω → (k → ℝ)}` | $X_i : \Omega \to \mathbb{R}^k$ | **Given:** the regressor sequence. |
| `{e : ℕ → Ω → ℝ}` | $e_i : \Omega \to \mathbb{R}$ | **Given:** the scalar error sequence. |
| `(h : ScoreCLTConditions μ X e)` | Hansen Assumption 7.2 | **Hypothesis:** the bundled score-CLT assumptions, linked on the [Chapter 7 assumption page](../../assumptions/chapter7.qmd). |
| `sampleCrossMoment ...` | $\hat g_n(e)=n^{-1}\sum_i X_i e_i$ | The sample score / cross moment built from the first $n$ observations. |
| `Real.sqrt (n : ℝ) • sampleCrossMoment ...` | $\sqrt n\,\hat g_n(e)$ | The CLT-scaled score. |
| `multivariateGaussian 0 (scoreCovarianceMatrix μ X e)` | $N(0,\Omega)$ | The centered Gaussian limit with score covariance matrix $\Omega$. |

## Tactic-by-tactic walkthrough

The vector theorem is short because all scalar projections have already been
proved.

::: {.lean-hover-panel}
```{=html}
<pre><code><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Start from the Euclidean-space version of the score CLT, already assembled by Cramer-Wold.">have hEuclid := scoreEuclidean_sampleCrossMoment_tendstoInDistribution_multivariateGaussian
    (μ := μ) (X := X) (e := e) h.toSampleCLTAssumption72</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Compose the Euclidean-valued convergence with the continuous coordinate map back into the chapter's k → ℝ vector notation.">have hMap := TendstoInDistribution.continuous_comp
    (g := (WithLp.ofLp : EuclideanSpace ℝ k → k → ℝ))
    (PiLp.continuous_ofLp 2 (fun _ : k => ℝ)) hEuclid</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="After unfolding function composition, the mapped theorem is exactly the public statement.">simpa [Function.comp_def] using hMap</span>
</code></pre>
```
:::

The upstream scalar covariance-form theorem is the piece closest to the
textbook calculation.

::: {.lean-hover-panel}
```{=html}
<pre><code><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="The covariance-form scalar theorem takes the Gaussian variance as a'Ωa.">theorem scoreProjection_sampleCrossMoment_tendstoInDistribution_gaussian_covariance … := by</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Restate the law of the limiting Gaussian in the variance form expected by the Mathlib CLT.">  have hZ' : HasLaw Z
      (gaussianReal 0 (Var[fun ω => (e 0 ω • X 0 ω) ⬝ᵥ a; μ]).toNNReal) ν := by</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Rewrite Var((eX)'a) as the quadratic form a'Ωa.">    rw [scoreProjection_variance_eq_quadraticScoreCovariance
      (μ := μ) (X := X) (e := e) h a]</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="The original Gaussian-law hypothesis now has the required shape.">    exact hZ</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Apply the sample-cross-moment scalar CLT with the rewritten Gaussian law.">  exact scoreProjection_sampleCrossMoment_tendstoInDistribution_gaussian
    (μ := μ) (ν := ν) (X := X) (e := e) h a hZ'</span>
</code></pre>
```
:::

## Downstream uses

The score CLT is the distributional input for Theorem 7.3's OLS asymptotic
normality and for the later t/Wald inference pages. In particular,
[`scoreProjection_olsBetaStar_tendstoInDistribution_gaussian_covariance`](theorem-7-3.qmd)
uses it after applying the inverse population Gram matrix and Slutsky's
theorem.
